Above and below the central content cluster, keep generous vertical whitespace so the page feels like a large white canvas with a compact center cluster of text and controls. The document viewer can extend downward and grow taller than the initial viewport, but its width remains constrained to the same centered column.
layout_strategies.content_density: Maintain very low content density. Each major element (heading, intro, search, navigation list, document viewer) is clearly separated by ample vertical spacing (roughly 2–3 line heights between blocks). The navigation links are spaced generously so that each link feels like an individual line item rather than part of a dense menu. No sidebars, no secondary panels, and minimal embellishment—mostly text with subtle rules or dividers only where needed.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: Follow the mockup’s Z-pattern at the top, then a vertical flow. The eye first encounters the dark header and existing site navigation (outside these components), then drops sharply into the high-contrast white content area where the centered 'Policies & Terms' title anchors attention. From there, the user reads a short description, notices the search form directly underneath as the first actionable control, then moves down into the vertical list of policy links. Selecting a link naturally leads the eye downward into the document viewer where the detailed policy text is presented. Any subtle loading indicator appears close to the document viewer’s top edge, so feedback is immediately associated with the viewed content.

layout.overall_description: Below the site’s existing dark header band, the page opens into a broad, quiet white space. In the middle of this space sits a relatively narrow, centered column, giving the impression of a formal document on a blank sheet. At the top of this column, the 'Policies & Terms' heading is center-aligned and positioned with substantial breathing room above and below, echoing the calm, conservative aesthetic of the mockup. Just under the title, a single, centered explanatory line introduces the page’s purpose. Beneath that, a modest, horizontally centered search form spans a bit over half the page width, visually anchored in the column with comfortable margins. Further down, a vertical list of policy titles appears, each as a separate, center-aligned line with generous spacing, similar to the numbered list styling seen in the mockup. Clicking a policy shifts focus downward to the document viewer, which occupies the full width of the same central column, presenting text in a clean, academic style with wide side margins. Invisible background processes—the loader and URL state manager—support this interaction without disrupting the minimalist visual. The overall effect is a restrained, text-centric page that feels structured and formal, with a clear top-to-bottom reading flow and abundant whitespace framing the content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The policy-documents-loader has no dedicated visible footprint. Conceptually, it resides behind the central content column, tied to the area where the policy-document-viewer appears. When a policy is loading, any user-facing indication (such as a small inline spinner or 'Loading policy…' label) appears at the top of the policy-document-viewer’s content area, centered horizontally within the content column and occupying only a single line of height, so it minimally disturbs the calm layout. It does not shift other elements laterally; it simply occupies the uppermost portion of the viewer where the document title or first lines of content would otherwise appear.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual infrastructure component; only indirectly expressed as a transient inline loading indicator at the top of the policy-document-viewer. It scrolls with the page as part of that viewer and does not use fixed or sticky positioning.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The policy-navigation-list is positioned in the main content column beneath the page title, intro line, and search form. It is center-aligned within the column and spans roughly 60% of the viewport width on desktop, keeping wide margins on both sides to preserve the text-centric, academic feel. Each policy item appears on its own line, aligned to the center, with generous vertical spacing—approximately the height of one extra line of text between items—similar to the separated numbering seen in the mockup. The entire list sits in the upper half of the white content area on initial load, so that the user can see the title and at least several navigation items without scrolling. When the document viewer content grows longer, the navigation list remains in its original position above, scrolling out of view in a simple linear top-to-bottom page flow.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, block-level component within the single centered column. It expands vertically as more policy items are added but keeps its width constrained; no sticky or floating behavior—when the user scrolls down to read a document, the list scrolls offscreen in the natural document flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The policy-search-form is placed directly beneath the 'Policies & Terms' title and any brief descriptive text, centered within the narrow content column. It spans approximately 55–65% of the viewport width on desktop, with the input field and submit button arranged on a single horizontal line. Above and below the form, there is generous vertical whitespace (roughly equivalent to 2–3 lines of body text), giving the search control space to breathe and avoiding visual clutter. The form’s alignment and width echo the central, text-centric layout from the mockup, appearing as a calm, understated tool rather than a dominant UI widget. It sits above the policy-navigation-list so that users discover search as an immediate method of locating policies before scanning the list of links.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static component centered within the main content column. It does not remain fixed when scrolling; it moves naturally with the page, eventually scrolling out of view as users move down into the document viewer.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The policy-document-viewer is positioned below the policy-navigation-list, aligned with and matching the same central content column. Its content area spans roughly the same width as the navigation list (about 60% of the viewport), ensuring very wide left and right margins that mimic the mockup’s narrow text column and academic reading feel. On initial page load, before any selection, the viewer can show a centered placeholder message, lightly framed in the middle of the lower half of the viewport, with substantial empty space above and below. Once a policy is selected, the document title appears at the top of this area, followed by body text arranged in a simple, flowing column. The viewer extends downward as needed, so the policy can be scrolled like a long document, but the width remains constant to preserve readability. The top of the viewer starts roughly at the lower third of the viewport on desktop, so that a user selecting an item from the navigation list naturally continues reading downward into the beginning of the policy content without abrupt layout changes.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, scrollable content region within the main page flow. It does not have its own inner scroll container; instead, the browser window scrolls the entire page. The viewer grows vertically according to the document length while respecting the fixed central column width.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The policy-url-state-manager is completely invisible and has no direct spatial presence on the page. Conceptually it resides at the level of the overall content column, linking the state of the policy-navigation-list and the policy-document-viewer with the browser’s URL. Its effect is only perceived when users bookmark or share a specific policy or when a deep-linked URL loads a particular policy directly into the viewer. There is no reserved space, border, or control associated with it; it simply ensures that the visible components reflect the correct state without altering the clean, minimal layout.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual, background controller with no layout footprint. It does not influence spacing or positioning of other components beyond triggering their content changes.
